news.summary: New Port Richey, Florida, April 18, 2025 - Zeo Energy Corp. (Nasdaq: ZEO) disclosed today that it was notified by Nasdaq on April 17, 2025, about its non-compliance with periodic filing requirements under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5250(c)(1). The company failed to file its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ending on December 31, 2024, with the SEC by the mandated deadline of March 31, 2025. This puts Zeo Energy at risk of losing its listing on the Nasdaq exchange.
news.headline: Zeo Energy Corp. (Nasdaq: ZEO) late in filing its Form 10-K for the year ended Dec 31, 2024; received Nasdaq notice.

news.summary: On April 18, 2025, CNFinance Holdings Limited (NYSE: CNF), a prominent Chinese home equity loan service provider, disclosed receiving a notification from the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) on April 7, 2025...
news.headline: CNFinance Holdings Limited notified of NYSE trading price requirement, received letter on April 7, 2025.

news.summary: The US Federal Reserve, led by Jerome Powell, is prepared to take decisive action to prevent a financial crisis, even amidst uncertainties like the US-led trade war. Last month, the Fed started easing financial conditions by reducing the redemption cap on Treasurys. This impacts global markets and cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin. Boston Fed President Susan Collins assured that the central bank could intervene to address liquidity issues if necessary. The approval of options trading for Ethereum ETFs is seen as a significant step to attract institutional capital. Canadian regulators have also approved staked Solana ETFs. Bitdeer, a Bitcoin mining company, is expanding its self-mining capacity in the US due to concerns over the trade war disrupting global supply chains. US President Trump's sons are investing in Bitcoin mining, aligning with the administration's focus on 'made in America' Bitcoin. Fed's next rate decision in June has a 67.5% likelihood of a cut. This week’s developments are highlighted in the Crypto Biz newsletter.
news.headline: US Fed under Powell gearing up to avoid crisis, eases policy with hints of potential rate cut. SEC approves options for spot ETH ETFs and Canada rolls out Solana staking ETFs. Bitdeer shifts focus to self-mining amid trade war concerns.

news.summary: The CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) hit a rare spike to 60 on April 7, signaling extreme market fear and uncertainty. This has only occurred five times in the last 35 years. Dan Tapiero, CEO of 10Tfund, believes this spike suggests a rebound for risk assets like Bitcoin in 6 to 12 months. The VIX is a "fear gauge" based on S&P 500 options trading, and spikes in 2008 and 2020 coincided with market bottoms. Julien Bittel of Global Macro Investor also supports Tapiero's claim, noting that tech stocks are oversold, similar to the 2008 financial crisis and the 2020 pandemic. The recent VIX spike indicates peak uncertainty, leading to fear among investors. The American Association of Individual Investors survey reflects high levels of bearish sentiment, similar to levels seen during past market bottoms. Despite potential bullish outcomes for risk assets, market analyst Tony Severino warns that the Bitcoin/VIX ratio may lead to a bear market. Bitcoin's peak may have occurred already, but this could change by the end of April. Severino's analysis suggests bearish conditions based on the Elliott Wave theory, indicating it is too early to predict a bullish turn for Bitcoin based on the VIX correlation. Readers are advised to conduct their research as investment decisions involve risks.
news.headline: Market fear spikes as VIX hits 60, pointing to possible Bitcoin surge in 6-12 months, says Dan Tapiero. Rare event seen only 5 times in 35 years.

news.summary: RBAZ Bancorp, Inc., the parent company of Republic Bank of Arizona, reported a consolidated net income of $1,064,000, or $0.59 per share, for the quarter ended March 31, 2025, a 49% increase from the previous year. Total loans increased by 0.6% to $223,962,000, while total deposits decreased by 3.7% to $240,864,000, partly offset by new banking relationships totaling $8,956,000. Total borrowings were $15,965,000, including short-term advances of $10,000,000. Total interest income increased to $4,485,000, and the Bank remained "Well Capitalized" with a CBLR ratio of 11.62%. The Company will merge with Pima Federal Credit Union on May 2, 2025. This marks the end of RBAZ's 18-year history as the top performing bank in Arizona.
news.headline: RBAZ Bancorp's Q1 2025 net income at $1,064,000, up 49% YoY, with total loans at $223,962,000 and deposits at $240,864,000.

news.summary: On April 18, 2025, in Omaha, Nebraska, Western Capital Resources, Inc. reported the conclusion of its tender offer for shares of its Common Stock. The offer came to an end at 5:00 P.M. on April 17, 2025, New York City time.
news.headline: Western Capital Resources, Inc. has concluded its tender offer for Common Stock, which expired on April 17, 2025 at 5:00 P.M.

news.summary: Signature Brands, the parent company of PAAS, a leading Easter Egg Coloring brand, will be organizing Easter Egg and Cookie Decorating Stations at the White House Easter Egg Roll event on April 21st. The event is set to take place in Washington, D.C. and is expected to attract over 30,000 attendees. Signature Brands, LLC will be providing interactive decorating stations for both eggs and cookies, offering a fun and creative experience for visitors. This initiative aims to engage participants in festive activities and enhance the overall Easter celebration.
news.headline: Signature Brands, LLC to continue 30+ year tradition of Easter decorating at White House Egg Roll with decorating stations.

news.summary: Attorney General Kwame Raoul revealed today that $24 million from the 2023 Juul Labs settlement will be allocated to support the nation's largest nonprofit focused on youth vaping prevention. This funding will be used to launch the "EX Program" campaign statewide in an effort to combat the rise of youth vaping.
news.headline: Juul settlement funds will bring $24 million for national youth vaping prevention to Illinois, led by Attorney General Raoul.

news.summary: The renowned fundraising organization, Colossal, has launched its yearly initiative to honor mothers and raise funds for Children's Miracle Network Hospitals. Beginning on April 18, 2025, in Phoenix, the nation's leading professional fundraiser, Colossal, revealed the commencement of voting for its celebrated Super Mom Competition.
news.headline: "Vote now for 2025 Super Mom Competition by Colossal, supporting Children's Miracle Network Hospitals. PHOENIX, April 18, 2025."

news.summary: Boca Raton, Florida, April 18, 2025 - US Claims Capital, LLC ("US Claims") has issued a notification regarding a data breach that could impact the privacy of specific individuals' data. The company is taking proactive measures to address this event and is disclosing details on the matter.
news.headline: US Claims Capital, LLC is notifying about a data event potentially affecting individuals' privacy. Take immediate action.

news.summary: Sentiment towards risk turned negative on Wednesday afternoon following comments by Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell linking increasing tariffs to higher inflation and slower economic growth. Powell stated that tariffs could have a more lasting impact on inflation than previously anticipated and the uncertainty surrounding current policies makes it challenging to assess the overall impact. Powell explicitly stated that the central bank has no plans to offset market losses, leading to a reassessment of expectations for immediate monetary support. This stance had a significant impact on Wall Street, particularly in relation to the belief in a potential "Fed put" to intervene during market declines. By 3:15 p.m. ET, the S&P 500, tracked by the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSE: SPY), had fallen by 3.1% to 5,230 points, with chipmakers notably extending losses. More detailed information can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Stocks plunge as Powell dismisses 'Fed put', gold surpasses Nasdaq. S&P 500 falls 3.1% to 5,230 points by 3:15pm.

news.summary: Illinois youth will benefit from $24 million allocated from the 2023 Juul Labs settlement to implement the EX Program and prevention curriculum. Attorney General Kwame Raoul revealed this initiative on April 18, 2025, aiming to decrease nicotine addiction and aid in cessation among the youth population in the state.
news.headline: IL to receive $24 million from Juul Labs settlement for youth vaping prevention programs, led by Truth Initiative.

news.summary: The Treasury Secretary, Scott Bessent, expressed his discontent to President Trump regarding the appointment of the acting commissioner without his awareness.
news.headline: IRS chief removed in conflict between Treasury's Bessent & Musk; Sec. Bessent informs Trump of unauthorized appointment.

news.summary: Fashion designer Conner Ives created a "Protect the Dolls" T-shirt to support a transgender cause. The shirt, worn by celebrities, quickly sold out online. The initiative has successfully raised funds for the cause amidst popularity.
news.headline: "Popular 'Protect the Dolls' tees by designer Conner Ives go viral, raising funds for trans cause. Celeb-endorsed, now sold out online."

news.summary: The first unlock event for the TRUMP token is set for April 18, releasing 40 million tokens, valued at $309 million, into circulation at a 90% discount from its peak. This represents 20% of the current circulating supply and could introduce volatility. The token has fluctuated between $7.46 and $7.83 in the past 24 hours, down 89.5% from its all-time high of $73.43. Over 800,000 wallets suffered losses totaling $2 billion after the token's value collapsed. The token's supply is mainly owned by Trump-affiliated organizations: CIC Digital LLC and Fight Fight Fight LLC own 80% of the tokens. The unlock event on April 18 is a large one-time release, with steadier unlocks planned afterwards. The emission schedule indicates that smaller unlocks will occur following this date. The token has generated controversy around conflicts of interest and insider trading concerns.
news.headline: TRUMP tokenholders face 90% loss from peak as 40M tokens unlock April 18, valued at $309M. Price swings $7.46-$7.83. CIC Digital & Fight Fight Fight own 80% supply. Losses estimated at $2B. Holders realize losses upon sale. Next unlocks will be steady. Trump's crypto ventures raise ethics questions.

news.summary: Starco Brands, Inc., based in Santa Monica, California, recently released a business update alongside its form 10-K filing for the fiscal year ending on December 31, 2024. The company, traded on OTCQB under the symbol STCB, specializes in creating and procuring innovative technologies and brands that inspire curiosity in daily life. Chairman & CEO Ross Sklar highlighted that the fourth quarter marked the culmination of a pivotal year marked by significant acquisitions and integration efforts.
news.headline: Starco Brands released 2024 results in fourth quarter and full year; CEO highlights transformative year.

news.summary: Scott Bessent, the Treasury Secretary, expressed his discontent to President Trump regarding the appointment of the acting commissioner, which had been made without his awareness.
news.headline: Treasury Secretary removes I.R.S. chief in dispute over appointment by Elon Musk; complaint lodged with President Trump.

news.summary: A recent report has indicated a significant increase in online shopping during the COVID-19 pandemic. The study revealed that online retail sales have surged by 75% compared to the previous year. This rise in online shopping can be attributed to the strict lockdown measures and social distancing protocols in place. The report also highlighted a notable shift towards e-commerce platforms, with a 60% increase in new online shoppers. Additionally, the data showed that the most popular categories for online purchases were electronics, home goods, and groceries. As more consumers turn to online shopping for their needs, experts predict that this trend is likely to continue in the coming months.
news.headline: US regulators have approved Capital One's acquisition of Discover in a deal worth billions.
UK's Chancellor Reeves advocates for deeper partnership with China - Telegraph.
UK's Chancellor Reeves: Cutting ties with China would be foolish -Telgraph

news.summary: Cryptocurrency exchange Kraken has introduced forex perpetual futures contracts to its Kraken Pro platform, offering exposure to global currency markets with 20x leverage and no expiry date. The first two contracts are euro-US dollar (EUR-USD) and British pound-US dollar (GBP-USD). Kraken previously added spot forex trading and signed a $1.5 billion deal to purchase NinjaTrader for offering crypto futures to US residents. It partnered with Mastercard for a crypto debit card and introduced stock and ETF trading in select US states. Kraken is eying a capital raise of up to $1 billion for a potential IPO in Q1 2026.
news.headline: Kraken now offers Forex perpetual futures with 20x leverage, including EUR-USD and GBP-USD contracts, expanding its financial product lineup.
Monday FX Options Expiries

news.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell expressed concerns over potential higher inflation and slower growth due to recent trade tariffs imposed by the Trump administration. Powell mentioned that growth has weakened in the first quarter of 2025, with inflation still slightly above the 2% objective. Surveys indicated declining sentiment among households and businesses, mainly due to uncertainties in trade policies prompting forecasters to adjust their outlooks. Despite these challenges, the majority of estimates point towards positive growth. Inflation rates, although cooling, are still above the Fed's target, with Total Personal Consumption Expenditures rising by 2.3% and Core PCE by 2.6%. Powell emphasized that inflation is decreasing gradually, and the impact of evolving policy changes remains uncertain.
news.headline: Fed Chair Powell rejects idea of Fed rescuing falling markets, hints at higher inflation. Growth softened Q1 2025, inflation above 2%. Trade tariffs causing uncertainty, sentiment decline. Inflation easing but still above 2%. Personal Consumption Expenditures rose 2.3%, Core PCE climbed 2.6%. Powell highlights policy changes' uncertain effects. Full story on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: A new report by BCC Research reveals that the Global EV Battery Swapping Market is expected to grow from $894.2 million in 2024 to $2.8 billion by the end of 2029, with a compound annual growth rate of 25.5%. The Asia-Pacific region leads the market, holding over 70% of the global share. The analysis covers various battery types, swapping stations, service types, and applications across different vehicle types. It also discusses emerging technologies, trends, market dynamics, and includes a patent analysis. Factors driving the market include rising global sales of electric vehicles, falling battery prices, and reduced upfront costs with battery swapping technology. The efficiency of battery swapping is beneficial for commercial fleets and city commuters needing quick turnaround. Additionally, the battery-as-a-service model is making EVs more accessible and promoting widespread adoption. For more details, the full story is available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Global EV Battery Swapping Market to grow from $894.2 million in 2024 to $2.8 billion by 2029, with Asia-Pacific leading at 70%. CAGR of 25.5%.

news.summary: A recent report indicates that the global average temperature has risen by 1.2 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels. This marks a significant increase in temperature, with scientists warning that a further increase of 0.5 degrees could have catastrophic consequences. The report also highlights that greenhouse gas emissions have reached record levels, with carbon dioxide levels in the atmosphere hitting 415 parts per million. This data emphasizes the urgent need for immediate action to curb emissions and limit further temperature rise to avoid irreversible damage to the environment.
news.headline: Macron aims to lure researchers to France amidst competition from the US.

news.summary: Chemung Financial Corporation reported a net income of $6.0 million in the first quarter of 2025, a slight increase from the previous quarter. The Corporation announced a 3.2% dividend increase to $0.32 per share. Net interest margin expanded to 2.96%, and commercial loan growth was at 10.5%. Total assets were $2.797 billion as of March 31, 2025, with total deposits at $2.433 billion. The Corporation's liquidity strategy includes short-term investments, deposits, and borrowings. Non-performing loans increased to $9.9 million, comprising 0.47% of total loans. Shareholder's equity was $228.3 million, and book value per share was $47.49. The Corporation operates 30 retail offices and focuses on community banking and wealth management services.
news.headline: Chemung Financial Corp Q1 2025 net income of $6.0M ($1.26 per share) increased from Q4 2024. Loan growth at 5.1%. Average interest-earning assets increased. Provision for credit losses also increased. Total assets at $2.797 billion. Non-interest income slightly down. Shareholders' equity is $228.3M. Market value of assets under management was $2.203 billion. Company maintains liquidity with cash, investments, and borrowing. Positive outlook given strategic initiatives.

The product is also known under the name... The recall was announced in Fremont, California, and reported through PRNewswire on April 18, 2025.
news.headline: FDA classifies Q'Apel Medical's recall of 1,617 units of 072 Aspiration System as Class I.

news.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) has been in a tight trading range around $83,000, and analysts suggest a breakout could lead to a new all-time high by Q3 or Q4 2025. Cryptollica projected a medium-term target of $155,000 for Bitcoin. Sygnum predicts a strong rally in altcoins due to improved regulations. Bitcoin must surpass $87,857 to climb to $95,000 and potentially $100,000. For Ethereum, crossing $1,754 could push it towards $2,111. XRP is consolidating near $2, with resistance at $2.23; BNB aims for $644 and SOL eyes $153. DOGE could hit $0.20 breaking $0.17, while ADA faces a test to stay above $0.59. LEO and Chainlink are also in pivotal zones, projecting potential resistance levels and drops. AVAX is balanced near $18.97, aiming for $23.50. Buyers need to break resistances for a bullish trend across multiple top cryptocurrencies.
news.headline: BTC, ETH, XRP price predictions for 4/18: Bitcoin to hit new ATH by Q3-Q4 2025. Sygnum bullish on altcoins rally in Q2. Source: Coin360.

news.summary: The University of Utah has revealed a groundbreaking donation of $25 million from The J. Willard and Alice S. Marriott Foundation on April 18, 2025 in Salt Lake City. This generous gift will be used to create a unique hub focused on hospitality innovation and leadership, a first of its kind at the university.
news.headline: University of Utah receives $25 million gift from The J. Willard and Alice S. Marriott Foundation to launch J.W. Marriott, Jr. Institute.

news.summary: On April 18, 2025, SparX Biopharmaceutical Corp disclosed plans to share clinical progress on SPX-303, their primary asset. As an innovative anti-LILRB2/PD-L1 bispecific antibody, SPX-303 updates will be presented at the Trial-in-Progress poster session of the 2025 American Association conference. This event is significant for the company as it provides a platform to showcase advancements in their groundbreaking treatment.
news.headline: SparX to share clinical updates on SPX-303, a dual checkpoint bispecific antibody, at AACR 2025 in Chicago.

news.summary: AUSTIN, TX - FeelWise, a brand that focuses on emotional intelligence, will be participating in the ATD 2025 International Conference & EXPO from May 18 to 21 in Washington, DC. This marks their debut as an exhibitor at the event. The company is based in Austin, Texas and the announcement was made on April 18, 2025.
news.headline: FeelWise will exhibit at ATD 2025 Expo in Washington, DC from May 18-21, as announced on April 18, 2025.

news.summary: In Tarrytown, N.Y., on April 18, 2025, Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. revealed that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has sent a complete response letter (CRL) concerning the supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) seeking to extend dosing intervals for EYLEA HD® (aflibercept) Injection 8 mg to up to every 24 weeks for all approved uses. The company's stock ticker, REGN on NASDAQ, was impacted by this announcement.
news.headline: Regeneron got FDA's complete response letter for extending EYLEA HD dosing to 24 weeks.$total of EYLEA HD Injection 8 mg.

news.summary: On April 18, 2025, in Elmira, N.Y., Chemung Financial Corporation (Nasdaq: CHMG), the parent company of Chemung Canal Trust Company, disclosed a net income of $6.0 million, translating to $1.26 per share in the first quarter of 2025. This figure shows a slight increase from the $5.9 million (or $1.24 per share) reported in the fourth quarter of 2024, but a decrease from the $7.1 million (or $1.48 per share) recorded in the first quarter of 2024.
news.headline: Chemung Financial Corp earned $6.0M in Q1 2025, $1.26 per share, up from Q4 2024's $5.9M and Q1 2024's $7.1M.

news.summary: During the shortened trading week before Easter, Wall Street experienced losses due to new export restrictions on chips to China. Nvidia Corp (NASDAQ: NVDA) received notification from the Trump administration on April 9 that its H20 AI chip would now need an export license to be sold in China. The company stated that these stricter controls could result in a $5.5 billion decrease in earnings. As a result, Nvidia shares fell by 8.5% for the week, wiping out nearly half of the previous week's gains. The broader semiconductor sector, as represented by the iShares Semiconductor ETF (NASDAQ: SOXX), also declined by 3%. This marks the seventh consecutive week of losses for the sector.
news.headline: Trump criticizes Powell, Nvidia plummets due to new export ban, gold prices surge. Nvidia's earnings could be cut by $5.5 billion, shares drop by 8.5%. Semiconductor sector dips by 3%, Nasdaq down.

news.summary: Federal prosecutors are still pursuing Braden John Karony, the former CEO of crypto firm SafeMoon, despite a memo from the US Justice Department suggesting a policy change related to digital assets. Karony faces securities fraud, wire fraud, and money laundering charges for allegedly misappropriating millions of dollars in 2021-2022. The DOJ memo hinted at a shift in crypto enforcement under Trump, with the SEC dropping cases against Ripple Labs, Coinbase, and others. Karony's trial is set for May, and he has pleaded not guilty. The SEC has taken a more permissive approach to digital assets under Trump, raising concerns about investor protection. Former SEC officials criticize the government's handling of crypto cases. The outcome of Karony's case remains uncertain.
news.headline: US prosecutors persist with ex-SafeMoon CEO case despite DOJ memo, indicting Braden John Karony for securities fraud, wire fraud, money laundering. Former CEO faces trial despite DOJ hinting at halting crypto enforcement. Source: PACER.

news.summary: As speculation arises over President Trump considering removing Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell before his term ends, experts predict potential consequences for Bitcoin and other markets.
news.headline: Trump's potential firing of Fed Chair Jerome Powell could have implications for Bitcoin and the US economy.
THEON releases its 2024 Annual Report with robust financials & key milestones. Strong growth expected in 2025.

news.summary: Legacy media and a small group of writers are trying to stir up controversy surrounding a question and positive remarks made by Washington, D.C. Chief Correspondent Cara Castronuova on Friday in the White House Press Briefing Room. Some reporters laughed while others were shocked and upset by the incident.
news.headline: Mike Lindell Media Corp. reporter Cara Castronuova causes a stir with a question for Karoline Leavitt on President Trump's health. Legacy media creates controversy.

news.summary: EyePoint Pharmaceuticals, Inc., headquartered in Watertown, Massachusetts, revealed on April 16, 2025, that they provided non-statutory stock options to fresh employees as inducement awards. These options were granted outside the Company’s 2023 Long-Term Incentive Plan, following NASDAQ Listing Rule 5635(c)(4). EyePoint Pharmaceuticals is dedicated to developing and marketing therapies for serious retinal diseases. The company operates under the ticker symbol EYPT on the Nasdaq stock exchange.
news.headline: EyePoint Pharma grants stock options under NASDAQ Rule to new employees, outside 2023 Plan. (Numbers not provided)
SUNation Energy plans to reverse split its stock in a 1-for-5 ratio, effective August 1.

news.summary: After a prolonged period of declining prices and negative charts, there has been a sudden turnaround in the oil market. The United States Oil Fund (NYSE: USO) has experienced a remarkable increase of over 7% in the past five days - marking its most substantial weekly gain in months. This surge has led to speculation regarding whether this signifies a possible end to the downward trend. However, despite this recent improvement, the USO ETF is still encountering challenges on a technical level. It currently remains below its 20, 50, and 200-day simple moving averages, indicating a prevailing bearish trajectory. Additionally, on the annual chart, the price hovers just below the 200-day SMA. The future direction of the market will largely depend on the continuation of buying momentum and bullish sentiment. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Oil prices have seen a sudden upswing, with US Oil Fund up by over 7% in the last five days, indicating a potential bottom. Trading below its SMAs, will it sustain the bullish momentum? Check Benzinga for details.

news.summary: Retail sales in March experienced a significant increase, with a surge in consumer purchases of automobiles and Apple iPhones ahead of the anticipated impact of Trump tariffs. This spike marked the largest jump in two years, contrasting with a decrease in the S&P 500.
news.headline: March retail sales surged, with consumers buying autos and iPhones ahead of Trump tariffs; S&P 500 declined.

news.summary: The Treasury Secretary, Scott Bessent, expressed his dissatisfaction to President Trump regarding the installation of the acting commissioner, which was done without his prior awareness.
news.headline: I.R.S. chief removed in Treasury vs. Elon Musk battle. Treasury Secretary objected to commissioner's appointment without his consent.

news.summary: In San Francisco, the prominent construction procurement platform, Kojo, revealed the introduction of an advanced set of AI-driven tools aimed at aiding contractors in mitigating the effects of escalating material expenses. In light of persistent pricing fluctuations influenced by international tariffs posing challenges to profit margins, Kojo's latest technology enables contractors to gain a competitive advantage by enhancing sourcing efficiency, intelligence, and cost efficiency. The innovative AI tools empower contractors to swiftly compare prices across multiple suppliers, ultimately streamlining the procurement process and bolstering their bottom line.
news.headline: Kojo launches AI tools to help contractors combat rising material costs, giving competitive edge with faster, smarter sourcing.

news.summary: Today, former Representative Lori Chavez-DeRemer is scheduled to testify before the Senate HELP Committee for her confirmation hearing. This marks a significant step in her journey toward potentially assuming a new role. The exact date and time of the hearing were not disclosed in the provided information.
news.headline: Former Rep. Lori Chavez-DeRemer will be at Senate HELP Committee for confirmation hearing today.

news.summary: Advocates for veterans are expressing growing concern over the Department of Government Efficiency's efforts to cut a significant number of federal jobs, a move that is expected to result in the loss of thousands of positions.
news.headline: Veterans and advocates affected as Department of Government Efficiency cuts thousands of federal jobs, causing disruption.

news.summary: Today, Democracy Forward is initiating public records requests to various federal agencies to inquire about the application of artificial intelligence in determining personnel choices.
news.headline: Critics request documents on AI use in federal agencies for personnel decisions. Democracy Forward to submit public records.

news.summary: The recent proposal by the Trump administration to sell numerous government properties, which includes the potential sale of hundreds of buildings such as the Frances Perkins Building, the current headquarters of the Labor Department, has brought attention to a concept that has been discussed for an extended period. This plan has emphasized the idea of selling off government-owned properties, with a significant number of properties being considered for sale. The Frances Perkins Building, which serves as the Labor Department’s headquarters, is among the properties that may be affected by this proposal.
news.headline: Trump administration planned to sell off hundreds of government properties, including DOL headquarters at Frances Perkins Building.
